ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 represents green tea as a liquid beverage; no Vietnam-specific green tea term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers green tea as a category; no Vietnam-specific sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 represents green tea as a substance; used as closest parent for this Vietnamese green tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of Vietnamese green tea, approximately 240 ml, served hot or at room temperature.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, green, brewed, prepared with tap water
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917) used as the closest approved source. Vietnamese green tea is a brewed green tea beverage; nutrient profile is essentially identical to standard brewed green tea. Values adapted directly from this entry. No Vietnam-specific green tea entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — plain brewed green tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates; GI effectively zero
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed green tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates (carbohydrate_g ~0.2 per 100g, essentially trace). The University of Sydney GI Database does not list a direct GI value for plain green tea because it contains insufficient carbohydrate to elicit a measurable glycemic response. By convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value), gi_category is 'low', and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. This is consistent with peer-reviewed literature on non-caloric tea beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (~1 mg per 100 g); contains catechins with mild vasodilatory properties
hypertension.guidance: Plain Vietnamese green tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its negligible sodium content (approximately 1 mg per 100 g) and the presence of catechins, which have been associated with modest reductions in blood pressure in observational studies; however, patients should avoid adding sugar or high-sodium additives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and sugar content; EGCG may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain Vietnamese green tea is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains negligible carbohydrates (~0.2 g per 100 g) and no added sugar, resulting in a glycemic load of effectively zero per serving; epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) in green tea has been associated with improved insulin sensitivity in clinical studies, though patients should consume it unsweetened.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; catechins and polyphenols associated with cardioprotective effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain Vietnamese green tea is beneficial for individuals with cardiovascular disease risk, as it contains zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and is rich in polyphenolic catechins that have been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and improved endothelial function in peer-reviewed cardiovascular research.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vietnamese Green Tea (Trà xanh Việt Nam) is a traditional brewed beverage made by steeping Vietnamese green tea leaves in hot water. Vietnam is one of the world's major green tea producers, with notable growing regions in Thai Nguyen, Moc Chau, and Lam Dong provinces. Vietnamese green tea is typically lighter and more delicate in flavour compared to Japanese or Chinese green teas, with a fresh, slightly grassy, and mildly astringent taste profile. It is commonly served plain, without milk or sugar, either hot or at room temperature. The beverage is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 g, with negligible protein (0.2 g), negligible carbohydrate (0.2 g), and zero fat. It is naturally rich in polyphenolic antioxidants, particularly catechins such as epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which have been studied for their anti-inflammatory, cardioprotective, and insulin-sensitising properties. Vietnamese green tea has a glycemic index effectively at zero, making it an ideal beverage for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease risk. In Singapore, Vietnamese green tea is served at Vietnamese restaurants and cafes, often as a complimentary or low-cost accompaniment to meals. It is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary requirements.
common_user_queries: Is Vietnamese green tea good for diabetes?
How many calories in Vietnamese green tea?
Is tra xanh the same as green tea?
Is Vietnamese green tea suitable for hypertension?
What are the health benefits of Vietnamese green tea?
Does Vietnamese green tea have sugar?
What is the GI of Vietnamese green tea?

confidence_reason: No Vietnam-specific green tea entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917), which is the closest approved source and is nutritionally equivalent to Vietnamese brewed green tea. GI is estimated by convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the use of an adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — plain brewed green tea contains no fat by nature. Confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171917). 2. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero for a plain brewed tea beverage. 3. sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero — no sugar is present in plain unsweetened brewed green tea. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero — brewed tea liquid contains no dietary fibre. 5. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per serving is negligible. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero for plain brewed green tea. 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant-based beverage contains no cholesterol. 8. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg are 0.0: confirmed zero or below detection threshold per USDA SR Legacy for brewed green tea. 9. calcium_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for brewed green tea. 10. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no Vietnam-specific green tea ontology term exists in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT. 11. This record is sourced from field survey data (Viet'Spice restaurant, Circular Road); the food itself is of Vietnamese origin and cuisine classification has been corrected from 'Singapore' to 'Vietnamese' to reflect the true cuisine of origin.

Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for fat_g, saturated_fat_g, sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g.

Documented all sentinel -1 replacements: replaced -1 values with 0.0 where USDA SR Legacy confirms zero or below detection threshold for brewed green tea; retained numeric values throughout per_100g.
